notes: The design/preflight/preview is useful as evidence of safety-by-design and traceability, but real SQL writes are not required to explain the TFG.
recommendation: Keep preview-only and frame as future-ready audit layer.

notes: A real writer would add persistence, but current artifacts already preserve the dry-run ledger reproducibly.
recommendation: Defer until runtime consumers genuinely need SQL persistence.

notes: Dashboard currently reads snapshots/artifacts/read-only SQL views, not runtime ledger writes.
recommendation: Do not block dashboard review on writer real.

notes: Telegram informational can summarize artifacts/previews; command bot is still pending and read-only.
recommendation: Do not use SQL writer as Telegram dependency now.

notes: Bot dry-run already produces CSV/JSON ledger and is validated artifact-first.
recommendation: Keep bot dry-run artifact ledger as canonical for now.

notes: AI Analyst could read artifacts first; SQL runtime may help later but is not necessary for design/read-only analysis.
recommendation: Do AI Analyst over artifacts/case packets first.

notes: Real writer introduces DB target, credentials, DDL/migrations, idempotency against live state and retention policy.
recommendation: Avoid now unless a strong consumer requires it.

notes: Moving beyond boolean checks introduces secret handling and DB safety risks.
recommendation: Keep environment/DB handling out of scope for now.

notes: Runtime SQL writes could make dry-run appear operational/live, especially with dashboard/Telegram nearby.
recommendation: Do not create real runtime writes before stronger UX/memory framing.

notes: A real runtime ledger can be mistaken as an execution queue before MT5 gates exist.
Current preview gives most methodological value with much lower operational risk.
recommendation: Recommend no-go for real writer now.
